signals.text: CHURNED ACCOUNT — brand marked churn_status:true in sentiment.json. All platform metrics are zero for the entire 45-day window (Feb 26 - Apr 12, 2026).

FERMAT API returned 'No funnel metrics found' for org 1e7aa370-3cde-40bf-90d9-8ce8a3aaed30 between Feb 26 and Apr 12, 2026. Zero sessions, revenue, purchases, pdp_views, atc, checkouts across all time windows.

Last recorded call was Dec 11, 2025 (123 days ago). No calls at all in 2026. Most recent call transcript reveals Tom Guilbert expressed strong churn signals: testing Webflow pages that outperform FERMAT, described $3,500/mo base fee as unsustainable, said brand is 'outgrowing the platform'.

Dec 5, 2025 call transcript confirms churn intent: Tom said 'we're exploring other solutions... it's pretty expensive on the long run' and reported paying $8,000-$9,000/mo. External Webflow pages were outperforming FERMAT funnels. Ryan Barry committed to return with new pricing options but left FERMAT on Dec 20.

CSM handoff failure: Ryan Barry left Dec 20, 2025. Isabel O'Keefe checked in Jan 16 with no reply from Tom. Mia DyBuncio joined channel Dec 19 but never posted. Pulkit Srivastava joined channel Feb 24 but never posted outreach. 3 CSM touches, zero brand responses.
Zero Gmail messages found for 'HAVN' in 30 days. No email communication whatsoever between FERMAT and HAVN in the recent window.
No upcoming calendar events with HAVN through Jun 12, 2026. Zero meeting cadence — brand has been completely dropped from the calendar since the CSM transition.

Tom Guilbert's last Slack message (Jan 6, 2026 — 97 days ago) reported a technical issue with the 50/50 incremental test split not working correctly. Issue resolution status unknown — 9 thread replies visible but no follow-up confirmation.

performance.data_note: FERMAT API returned 'No funnel metrics found' for org 1e7aa370-3cde-40bf-90d9-8ce8a3aaed30 between 2026-02-26 and 2026-04-12. Brand is fully churned with zero funnel traffic. No daily rows to aggregate.

recent.title: HAVN x Fermat Weekly
recent.summary: Final weekly sync before Ryan Barry's departure. Isabel O'Keefe introduced as Head of AM. Tom and Ryan discussed ongoing funnel performance, AI Search trial activation (Tom confirmed setup complete, zero results so far), and blanket PDP updates with new colors/fabrics. Ryan committed to continued support through handoff. This was the last active engagement call before the brand went silent.
next_steps: Ryan handoff to Mia DyBuncio and Isabel O'Keefe for ongoing account management
Tom to continue monitoring AI Search trial results
Tom to update blanket PDP with new colors and fabric types

recent.title: HAVN x Fermat Weekly
recent.summary: CRITICAL CHURN SIGNAL CALL. Tom disclosed HAVN is testing external Webflow pages that outperform FERMAT funnels, reduced platform traffic significantly. Key pain points: (1) $3,500/mo base fee is unsustainable — 'either you go with it or you don't, there's no middle ground'; (2) Monthly costs hit $8,000-$9,000 which equals 'a full-time creative strategist'; (3) Design limitations — 'we can't get a designer to just go on the format page'; (4) Desktop limitations; (5) Feels they are 'outgrowing the platform'. Ryan committed to return with revised commercial options. Tom confirmed AI Search is set up. Brand explicitly considering cancellation.
next_steps: Ryan to circle up with team and return with revised commercial options / pricing tiers
Follow-up meeting scheduled for Dec 11 at 10:30 AM PT
Tom to continue testing Webflow vs FERMAT pages for blanket product
churn_signals: Testing Webflow pages that outperform FERMAT
Monthly costs $8,000-$9,000 seen as disproportionate to profit
Brand feels they are 'outgrowing the platform'
Explicitly said 'I could also just told you out of the blue in a few weeks that we're just going to stop'

Pre-BFCM preparation context.
next_steps: Tom to test sticky CTA on main beanie funnel immediately

calls.analysis: HAVN had active weekly calls from Oct through Dec 2025 with Ryan Barry as primary CSM. Ryan departed FERMAT on Dec 20, 2025. The Dec 5 call is the most critical — full transcript reveals Tom Guilbert explicitly expressing churn intent: testing Webflow alternatives that outperform FERMAT, describing the $3,500/mo base fee as unsustainable, and stating the brand is 'outgrowing the platform'. Ryan committed to return with revised pricing options on Dec 11 but then left the company 9 days later. Isabel O'Keefe was introduced on the Dec 11 call but no follow-through on pricing revision occurred. No calls recorded in 2026 at all — 123 days of complete silence since Dec 11, 2025. Brand contact: Tom Guilbert (tom@havnwear.com). CRM Deal ID: 006Rm00000YgVn0IAF.

calendar.analysis: Google Calendar search for 'HAVN' from Mar 14 to Jun 12, 2026 returned zero events. No past or upcoming meetings scheduled. The brand has been completely dropped from the calendar since the CSM transition. The promised pricing revision follow-up from Dec 5 call was never scheduled after Ryan's departure.

slack.analysis: Slack channel #havn-x-fermat (C08SNU8SHB6) shows a clear pattern of account decay. Ryan Barry was the active CSM through Dec 2025 with consistent, high-quality engagement (product updates, post-call follow-ups, proactive outreach). After Ryan's departure on Dec 20, 2025: (1) Mia DyBuncio joined Dec 19 but never posted; (2) Isabel O'Keefe sent one check-in on Jan 16 — no reply from Tom; (3) Tom's last message was Jan 6, 2026 (97 days ago) reporting a technical bug; (4) Pulkit joined Feb 24 but never posted outreach. The brand contact Tom Guilbert has been completely silent since Jan 6, 2026. Total FERMAT-side messages since Ryan left: 2 (Isabel check-in + Pulkit join). Total brand-side messages since Ryan left: 1 (Tom's bug report). Last brand engagement: 97 days ago.

email.analysis: Gmail search for 'HAVN newer_than:30d' returned zero results. No email communication between FERMAT and HAVN in the past 30 days. This confirms complete communication blackout across all channels.
